filename: GHM Community Voices Display -Jewish American Heritage Month.pdf
title: Community Voices Display Flyer
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Document promoting the Greensboro History Museum Community Voices Display for Jewish American Heritage Month. The document is calling for the Greensboro Jewish community to temporarily loan objects to fill the display.
subject: Judaism; Local History; Museum Exhibits

title: Beth David Synagogue Original Location
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Photograph of the original location of Beth David Synagogue. The groundbreaking for the building occurred in 1947, and the congregation moved out in 1982. The building is now owned by Faithway Baptist Church.
subject: Judaism; Local History

title: Greensboro History Museum Display Case
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Photograph of a display case at the Greensboro History Museum. There are very few Jewish items in the GHM collection; on display in their permanent exhibit are a Kiddush cup and a Sefer Torah.
subject: Judaism; Local History; Museum Exhibits

title: Miriam B. Brenner Children's Museum Entrance
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Photograph of the entrance sign to the Miriam P. Brenner Children's Museum. Formerly known as the Greensboro Children's Museum, the museum officially opened on May 15, 1999.
subject: Museums; Jewish History

title: She Wouldn't Take Off Her Boots Monument
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Photograph of Victoria Milstein's sculpture "She Wouldn't Take Off Her Boots" in LeBaeur Park. This sculpture is North Carolina's first and only women's Holocaust monument, unveiled in April 2023.
subject: Holocaust; Memorial Monuments

title: Elsewhere Entrance
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Photograph of the entrance to the Elsewhere museum. Formerly owned by Sylvia Gray as a surplus thrift store, it has been transformed into a museum and artist residency using the 60-year collection of materials from the original second-hand store.
subject: Jewish History; Museums; Business

title: North Carolina Museums Conference Poster
creator: Kennedy Gray
description: Poster created by second-year MA museum studies student Kennedy Gray for the North Carolina Museums Conference in Greenville, NC. The poster, "Stepping Stones: Physical Meets Digital Through Greensboro Jewish History Tour," details how other institutions can engage with communities to create dynamic, accessible tours, using a case study of the Greensboro Jewish history walking tour.
subject: Jewish History; Museums and Commnities
